metaDescription: Overview of the different ways to export data out of Jira, from issue lists to full project backups.
intro: Jira offers several export paths depending on what you actually need — a quick spreadsheet of issues, a full project backup, or data pulled through the API. This guide walks through the main options.

steps.heading: Decide what you need to export
steps.body: Figure out whether you need a handful of issues, an entire project, or just specific fields before choosing an export method.

steps.heading: Use issue search export for smaller sets
steps.body: Run a search or filter, then use the export button to download matching issues as Excel or CSV.

steps.heading: Use project export for a full backup
steps.body: Jira admins can export an entire project's data as a backup file, useful for archiving or migration.

steps.heading: Consider the REST API for custom exports
steps.body: For more control over fields and format, query Jira's REST API and process the response into whatever file type you need.

steps.heading: Verify the exported data
steps.body: Open the resulting file to confirm it includes everything expected before relying on it.

faq.question: Can I export an entire Jira project at once?
faq.answer: Yes, Jira administrators can run a full project export, typically used for backups or migrations.

faq.question: What file formats does Jira support for exports?
faq.answer: Common formats include CSV and Excel for issue lists, along with XML or JSON for more technical exports via the API.

faq.question: Is exporting data the same as backing it up?
faq.answer: Not exactly; a targeted issue export is a snapshot of specific data, while a full backup captures the entire project structure.

faq.question: Do I need special permissions to export Jira data?
faq.answer: Basic issue exports usually just require view access, while full project exports need administrator permissions.
